history_text: 64-year-old male with a 5-year history of Type 2 diabetes mellitus, currently poorly controlled (HbA1c 9.9%). Comorbidities include stage 3 chronic kidney disease, hyperlipidaemia. Renal function is mildly to moderately reduced (eGFR 69.3 mL/min/1.73m²). Current medications include Metformin 500mg twice daily, Insulin NPH 22 units nightly, Amlodipine 5mg once daily. No known drug allergies. He denies recent hospitalisations or surgical procedures.
